channel.title: Job on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Job on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Job 1
item.description:
Job Chapter 1

Conversational Yahveh Covenant

1 Job lived in the land of Uz, a blameless, godly man that respected God and shunned evil. 2 He had seven sons and three daughters; 3 and owned 7,000 sheep, 3,000 camels, 500 yoke of oxen, 500 female donkeys; and many servants. 4 His sons would hold feasts in each other’s houses and invite their three sisters. 5 When the feasting days had completed their cycle, Job would consecrate them, getting up early and offering burnt offerings for each one, “Maybe my children have sinned and cursed God in their hearts.”

item.title: Job 2
item.description:
Job Chapter 2

1 On another day when the sons of God came to present themselves to Yahveh, Satan came with them. 2 Yahveh asked him,

“Where’d you come from?”

“From roaming around in the world.”

3 “Have you noticed my servant Job? There’s nobody like him in the world, a blameless, godly man that respects me and shuns evil. He still maintains his integrity, even though you incited me against him to ruin him for no reason.”

item.title: Job 3
item.description:
Job Chapter 3

1 Afterward Job cursed the day he was born: [ 2 ]

3 “Perish the day I was born,

                        the night I was conceived.

                  4 May that be a dark day;

                        let God above not care about it

item.title: Job 4
item.description:
Job Chapter 4

I’m not at rest; only trouble is coming.”              Job 3:20-26

1 Eliphaz the Temanite answered:

                  2 “Will you be offended if someone offers you advice?

                        Who can help speaking up?

                  3 You’ve admonished many people

                        and encouraged the weak.

                  4 What you’ve said has helped the tottering stand

item.title: Job 5
item.description:
Job Chapter 5

They die in ignorance.’                             Job 4:12-21

              5:1“Call out for help now; will anybody answer you?

                        Which of the angels will you turn to?

                  2 Adversity slays the foolish;

                        anger kills the simpleminded.

                  3 I’ve seen the foolish taking root

item.title: Job 6
item.description:
Job Chapter 6

1 Job answered:

                  2 “Oh that my adversity was properly weighed

                              in the balances with my sins!

                        3 It would outweigh the sand by the seashore;

                              that’s why I’ve been rash.

                  4 The arrows of the Almighty are piercing me;

item.title: Job 7
item.description:
Job Chapter 7

Can’t I distinguish guilt from disaster?               Job 6:24-30

              7:1“Aren’t people forced into hard labor?

                        Aren’t their days like those of a hired hand?

                  2 Like a slave panting for shade,

                        like an employee waiting for wages,

                        3 I’ve been allotted months of emptiness

item.title: Job 8
item.description:
Job Chapter 8

1 Bildad the Shuhite answered:

                  2 “How long will you talk like that?

                        You make your mouth a blustering wind?

                  3 Does God pervert justice?

                        Does the Almighty twist the truth?

                  4 When your children sinned against him,

item.title: Job 9
item.description:
Job Chapter 9

1 Job answered:

                  2 “I know that’s true,

                        but how can anyone be innocent in God’s sight?

                  3 If you wanted to dispute with him,   

                        you couldn’t answer him once in a thousand times.

                  4 He’s so wise and powerful;

item.title: Job 10
item.description:
Job Chapter 10

              10:1“I detest my life;

                        so I’ll fully vent my complaint

                        and speak out of my total frustration.

                  2 I’ll tell God, ‘Don’t just condemn me;

                        tell me why you’re contending with me.

                  3 Is it right for you to oppress me, to reject my toil,

item.title: Job 11
item.description:
Job Chapter 11

1 Zophar the Naamathite answered:

                  2 “Should your barrage of words go unchallenged,

                        and you exonerate yourself by talking a lot?

                  3 Will your babble shut people up?

                        Will you mock and nobody rebuke you?

                  4 You claim your beliefs are pure

item.title: Job 12
item.description:
Job Chapter 12

1 Job answered:

                  2 “No doubt, you’re the people,

                        and wisdom will die with you!

                  3 But I’m as smart as you are;

                        I’m not your inferior.

                        Who doesn’t know things like that?

                  4 I’m a laughingstock to my friends,

item.title: Job 13
item.description:
Job Chapter 13

              13:1“I’ve seen that;

                        I’ve heard it and understand it.

                  2 What you know, I know too;

I’m not more ignorant than you.                          Job 13:1-2

                  3 “But I’m willing to speak to the Almighty himself;

                        I want to argue with God.

item.title: Job 14
item.description:
Job Chapter 14

              1 “Mortals born of women

                        are short-lived and filled with turmoil.

                  2 They come out and wither like a flower;

                        they pass away like a shadow and don’t last.

                  3 You keep your eyes open at them

item.title: Job 15
item.description:
Job Chapter 15

1 Eliphaz the Temanite answered:

                  2 “Should you answer with empty talk

                        and fill yourself with the east wind?

                  3 Should you argue with useless speech,

                        with words that do no good?

                  4 You’re losing reverence for God

item.title: Job 16
item.description:
Job Chapter 16

1 Job answered:

                  2 “I’ve heard a lot of things like that;

                        you’re miserable comforters.

                  3 Is there no end to windy words?

                        What’s your problem that you answer me like that?

                  4 I could talk to you like you’re talking if I was in your place.

item.title: Job 17
item.description:
Job Chapter 17

                  1 My spirit’s broken; my days are snuffed out;

                        people have already dug my grave.

                  2 Mockers sit around

                        and taunt me to my face.                                      

Job 16:18-17:2

                  3 “God, sign a pledge for me;

item.title: Job 18
item.description:
Job Chapter 18

1 Bildad the Shuhite answered:

                  2 “How long will you stumble around for words?

                        Make some sense so we can talk.

                  3 Why do you treat us like animals?

                        You think we’re stupid?

                  4 Are you going to tear yourself to pieces in your anger?

item.title: Job 19
item.description:
Job Chapter 19

1 Job answered:

                  2 “How long are you going to torment me

                        and shatter me with a barrage of words?

                  3 You’ve insulted me ten times over;

                        aren’t you ashamed of treating me wrong?

                  4 Even if I did make mistakes,

item.title: Job 20
item.description:
Job Chapter 20

1 Zophar the Naamathite answered:

                  2 “My thoughts make me answer

                        because I’m in turmoil inside.

                  3 I’ve listened to your insults,

                        and I’m answering from what I understand.

                  4 Don’t you know from of old—

item.title: Job 21
item.description:
Job Chapter 21

1 Job answered:

2 “Listen carefully;

let this be your consolation.

3 Bear with me;

after I’ve spoken, you can mock me.

4 Am I just complaining about people?

Why shouldn’t I be impatient?

5 Be appalled at the way I look;

item.title: Job 22
item.description:
Job Chapter 22

Your explanations don’t make any sense.”          Job 21:30-34

1 Eliphaz the Temanite answered.

2 “What benefit can a mortal be to God?

Aren’t even the wise only useful to themselves?

3 Is the Almighty delighted if you’re good?

What good is it to him if you’re perfect?

4 Surely it’s not because you revere him that he’s reproving you,

item.title: Job 23
item.description:
Job Chapter 23

Job 22:21-30

1 Job answered:

2 “My complaint is still bitter;

God’s hand weighs heavily on me despite my groaning.

3 If only I knew where to find him

so I could go to his courtroom!

4 I’d present my case

and offer my arguments.

item.title: Job 24
item.description:
Job Chapter 24

1 “Why doesn’t the Almighty maintain

a regular court schedule for judging?

Why do those who know him wait for him in vain?

2 Some remove landmarks,

seize flocks and eat them,

3 drive away donkeys that belong to the fatherless,

take widows’ oxen for pledges.

item.title: Job 25
item.description:
Job Chapter 25

1 Bildad the Shuhite answered:

2 “Dominion and awe belong to God,

who establishes order high in his sky.

3 Who can count his troops?

Who doesn’t his light rise on?

4 How can anyone be innocent in his presence?

How can anyone born of woman be pure?

item.title: Job 26
item.description:
Job Chapter 26

1 Job answered:

2 “What a help you are to the weak!

How you’ve saved arms with no strength!

3 What counsel you’ve given me in my stupidity!

What brilliant insight you’ve provided!

4 Who’ve you been talking to?

Whose spirit has been speaking through you?!    Job 26:1-4

5 “The departed spirits tremble

item.title: Job 27
item.description:
Job Chapter 27

Who can comprehend his power?”          Job 26:5-14

1 Job continued his comments:

2 “As God lives, who has taken away my rights,

the Almighty, who has made me bitter,

3 as long as I’m alive and God’s breath is in me,

4 I won’t speak wickedness or lie.

item.title: Job 28
item.description:
Job Chapter 28

1 “There’s a mine for silver

and a refinery for gold.

2 Iron comes out of the ground,

and people can smelt copper.

3 They use a torch in the darkness

and go in as far they can,

searching for the ore in gloom and deep shadow.

item.title: Job 29
item.description:
Job Chapter 29

1 Job took up his discourse again:

2 “Oh that I could be like I was just a few months ago

when God was watching over me,

3 when his lamp shined above my head

and I walked through darkness by his light,

4 while I was in the prime of life

item.title: Job 30
item.description:
Job Chapter 30

         30:1“But now people younger than me make fun of me,

people whose fathers I wouldn’t let

run with my sheep dogs.

2 What good was their strength to me,

fellows whose vigor was gone?

3 They were haggard from hunger and want

item.title: Job 31
item.description:
Job Chapter 31

1 “I’ve made a covenant with my eyes

not to look with lust at a young woman?

2 What is our portion from God above,

our inheritance from the Almighty on high?

3 Isn’t it misfortune for the unjust,

disaster for sinners?

4 Doesn’t he see everything I do

item.title: Job 32
item.description:
Job Chapter 32

Job’s words end here.                                                     Job 31:38-40

1 The three men stopped replying to Job, because he was certain he was innocent. 2 Then Elihu Ben-Barachel the Buzite from the family of Ram, was mad at Job because he’d justified himself to God. 3 He was mad at his three friends because they couldn’t answer Job but still condemned him. 4 Elihu had waited to speak because they were years older than he was. 5 When he saw they didn’t provide an answer, his anger burned inside him. 6 So he spoke up.

item.title: Job 33
item.description:
Job Chapter 33

1 “But now, Job, listen to what I have to say;

hear me out.

2 I’m opening my mouth now;

the tongue in my mouth is speaking.

3 What I’m saying expresses my uprightness;

my lips declare knowledge sincerely.

4 The Spirit of God has made me;

item.title: Job 34
item.description:
Job Chapter 34

1 Elihu continued:

2 “Listen, you wise men;

pay attention, you that ‘know’;

3 the ear tests words

like the palate tastes food.

4 Let’s choose what’s right;

let’s determine for ourselves what’s good;

5 because Job has said, ‘I’m good,

item.title: Job 35
item.description:
Job Chapter 35

1 Elihu continued:

2 “Do you think it’s righteous

for you to say you’re upright before God?

3 You say, ‘What advantage will I have?

What benefit will I get more than if I’d sinned?’

4 I’ll answer you and your friends.

5 Look at the sky;

item.title: Job 36
item.description:
Job Chapter 36

1 Elihu continued:

2 “Bear with me a little longer, and I’ll show you

what I have to say on God’s behalf.

3 I’ll bring my knowledge from afar

and credit goodness to my Maker.

4 What I’m saying is true;

I know perfectly well what I’m talking about.

item.title: Job 37
item.description:
Job Chapter 37

1 “My heart pounds when I think about this;

it feels like it’s out of place.

2 Listen closely to his thundering,

the rumbling that comes from his mouth.

3 He unleashes his lightning under the whole sky;

it travels to the horizon in every direction.

item.title: Job 38
item.description:
Job Chapter 38

Job 37:14-24

1 God answered Job out of the whirlwind:

2 “Who is this that confuses my counsel

with ignorant words?

3 Gird up* like a man;

I’ll ask you some questions, and you answer me.

4 Where were you when I laid the foundation for the earth?

item.title: Job 39
item.description:
Job Chapter 39

1 “Do you know when mountain goats give birth?

Do you ever see a deer calving?

2 Can you count the months of their gestation

or learn when they have their fawns?

3 They crouch down to deliver their young.

4 Their offspring grow strong out in the open;

item.title: Job 40
item.description:
Job Chapter 40

1 Yahveh said to Job,

2 “Will a faultfinder spar with the Almighty?

Let the one that argues with God answer that.”

3 Job said,

4 “I’m insignificant; what kind of answer can I give?

I’ve put my hand over my mouth.

5 I’ve spoken once or twice; that’s enough;

item.title: Job 41
item.description:
Job Chapter 41

Can anybody trap it and put a ring in its nose?   Job 40:19-24

         41:1“Can you pull Leviathan in on a fishhook

or muzzle it with a noose?

2 Can you tie it up with a rope in its nose

or pierce its jaw with a hook?

3 Will it beg you for mercy

item.title: Job 42
item.description:
Job Chapter 42

1 Job answered Yahveh:

                  2 “I know you can do anything

                        and nobody can thwart what you do.

                  3 You asked, ‘Who is this that darkens my counsel with ignorance?’

                              So I admit I don’t understand;

                              these things are too awesome for me to look into.
